name: Selection
summary: A set of the currently selected drawing elements. Use the Model.selection method to get a Selection object. Note that the order of drawing elements (selection[0], selection[1] and so on) in the set is in no particular order and should not be assumed to be in the same order as the user selected the drawing elements.

methods.summary: The #[] method is used to retrieve a Drawingelement from the selection by index.
methods.remarks: The #[] method is used to retrieve a Drawingelement from the selection by index. Index 0 is the first Drawingelement in the selection. This method is not very efficient. If you need to look at every entity in the selection, consider using #each instead of using this method to manually grab each one.

params.name: index
params.doc: The index of the Drawingelement object to retrieve.

methods.name: add
methods.summary: The #add method is used to add Drawingelement to the selection.
methods.remarks: The #add method is used to add Drawingelement to the selection. Drawingelements that are added to the Selection are visually indicated by the selection bounding box. You can pass it individual Drawingelements or an Array of Drawingelements.

returns.doc: the number of Drawingelement objects added

methods.name: add_observer
methods.summary: The add_observer method is used to add an observer to the selection object.
methods.remarks: The add_observer method is used to add an observer to the selection object.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: at
methods.summary: The #at method is an alias for #[].
methods.remarks: The #at method is an alias for #[].

params.name: index
params.doc: The index of the Drawingelement object to retrieve.

methods.name: clear
methods.summary: The clear method is used to clear the selection.
methods.remarks: The clear method is used to clear the selection.

methods.name: contains?
methods.summary: The #contains? method is and alias of #include?.
methods.remarks: The #contains? method is and alias of #include?.

methods.name: count
methods.summary: Note: Since SketchUp 2014 the count method is inherited from Ruby's Enumerable mix-in module.
methods.remarks: Note: Since SketchUp 2014 the count method is inherited from Ruby's Enumerable mix-in module. Prior to that the #count method is an alias for #length.

methods.name: each
methods.summary: Note: Don't remove content from this collection while iterating over it with #each.
methods.remarks: Note: Don't remove content from this collection while iterating over it with #each. This would change the size of the collection and cause elemnts to be skipped as the indices change. Instead copy the current collection to an array using to_a and then use each on the array, when removing content. The #each method is used to iterate through all of the selected Drawingelements. If you want to do something with all of the selected Drawingelements, this is more efficient than using #[].

methods.name: empty?
methods.summary: The #empty? method is used to determine if there are drawing elements in the selection.
methods.remarks: The #empty? method is used to determine if there are drawing elements in the selection.

methods.name: first
methods.summary: The #first method is used to retrieve the first selected Drawingelement Returns nil if nothing is selected.
methods.remarks: The #first method is used to retrieve the first selected Drawingelement Returns nil if nothing is selected. This method is useful when you know that only a single Drawingelement is selected, or you are only interested in the first selected Drawingelement.

returns.doc: the first selected Drawingelement object if successful

methods.name: include?
methods.summary: The #include? method is used to determine if a given Drawingelement is in the selection.
methods.remarks: The #include? method is used to determine if a given Drawingelement is in the selection.

methods.name: invert
methods.summary: The #invert method is used to invert the selection.
methods.remarks: The #invert method is used to invert the selection.

methods.name: is_curve?
methods.summary: The #is_curve? method is used to determine if the selection contains all edges that belong to a single curve.
methods.remarks: The #is_curve? method is used to determine if the selection contains all edges that belong to a single curve.

methods.name: is_surface?
methods.summary: The #is_surface? method is used to determine if the selection contains only all of the faces that are part of a single curved surface.
methods.remarks: The #is_surface? method is used to determine if the selection contains only all of the faces that are part of a single curved surface.

methods.name: length
methods.summary: The #length method is used to retrieve the number of selected drawing elements.
methods.remarks: The #length method is used to retrieve the number of selected drawing elements.

methods.name: model
methods.summary: The #model method retrieves the model for the selection.
methods.remarks: The #model method retrieves the model for the selection.

returns.doc: the model that includes the selection if successful

methods.name: nitems
methods.summary: The #nitems method is an alias for #length.
methods.remarks: The #nitems method is an alias for #length.

methods.name: remove
methods.summary: The #remove method is used to remove Drawingelements from the selection.
methods.remarks: The #remove method is used to remove Drawingelements from the selection. You can pass it individual Drawingelements or an Array of Drawingelements.

returns.doc: the number of Drawingelement objects removed

methods.name: remove_observer
methods.summary: The remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the selection object.
methods.remarks: The remove_observer method is used to remove an observer from the selection object.

returns.doc: true if successful, false if unsuccessful.

methods.name: shift
methods.summary: The #shift method is used to remove the first Drawingelement from the selection and returns it.
methods.remarks: The #shift method is used to remove the first Drawingelement from the selection and returns it.

returns.doc: the first Drawingelement object in the selection set if successful

methods.name: single_object?
methods.summary: The #single_object? method is used to determine if the selection contains a single object.
methods.remarks: The #single_object? method is used to determine if the selection contains a single object. It can either be a single DrawingElement or a group of DrawingElements for which is_curve? or is_surface? will return true.

methods.name: size
methods.summary: The #size method is an alias for #length.
methods.remarks: The #size method is an alias for #length.

methods.name: toggle
methods.summary: The #toggle method is used to change whether a Drawingelement is part of the selection.
methods.remarks: The #toggle method is used to change whether a Drawingelement is part of the selection. Drawingelements that are not already selected are added. Drawingelements that are already selected are removed. You can pass it individual Drawingelements or an Array of Drawingelements.

returns.doc: the number of Drawingelement objects changed
